identity.name: Linux Defense Evasion — Log Tampering, Timestomping, Masquerading (T1070.002)
identity.summary: An operator covers tracks on a Linux host: clearing bash history, truncating/accessing auth.log and syslog, timestomping with touch -t/-r, copying a shell to a kernel-thread name to masquerade in process listings, and enumerating EDR/AV agents to plan tamper. Cortex XDR for Linux detects each anti-forensics action via command-line and file BIOCs and stitches them into one defense-evasion incident.
identity.description: Defense evasion is where attackers buy dwell time. Unit 42's xHunt campaign showed operators tampering with logs and disguising tooling to frustrate responders. This scenario reproduces five Linux anti-forensics techniques: bash history clearing via HISTFILE manipulation and history -c, system log access and truncation against /var/log/auth.log and syslog, file timestamp manipulation with touch -t and touch -r, process masquerading by copying /bin/sh to a kernel-thread-style name like [kworker/0:1], and security-tool reconnaissance by grepping the process table for falcon/crowd/cortex/sentinel agents. The detection approach is command-line behavioral analysis plus file-write telemetry: history/log/timestomp/masquerade actions each map to a Cortex XDR BIOC, an analytics layer recognizes the anti-forensics sequence, and a correlation rule fuses the activity into a single defense-evasion incident. The security-tool enumeration step is deliberately a discovery behavior often missed by default that the custom BIOC closes — exactly the gap-coverage POV narrative.

techniques.name: Indicator Removal: Clear Linux or Mac System Logs
Masquerading: Match Legitimate Name or Location
Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools

execution.framework_reference_id: T1070.002 / T1070.003 / T1070.006 / T1036.005 — Linux anti-forensics atomic tests

prerequisites.description: Cortex XDR Linux agent installed and reporting healthy on the target.
prerequisites.check_command: systemctl is-active cortex-agent || ps aux | grep -i traps

prerequisites.description: Root privilege to access /var/log and modify file timestamps.

payload.code:
# step-1: clear bash history
export HISTFILE=/dev/null; unset HISTFILE; history -c 2>/dev/null; echo '' > ~/.bash_history
# step-2: tamper system logs
cp /var/log/auth.log /tmp/cortexsim_auth_backup 2>/dev/null; echo '' > /tmp/cortexsim_fake_auth.log; wc -l /var/log/syslog /var/log/auth.log 2>/dev/null
# step-3: timestomp
touch -t 202301010000 /tmp/cortexsim_timestomp_test; touch -r /usr/bin/ls /tmp/cortexsim_timestomp_test
# step-4: masquerade
cp /bin/sh /tmp/cortexsim_masq_test; mv /tmp/cortexsim_masq_test '/tmp/[kworker/0:1]' 2>/dev/null
# step-5: enumerate security tools
ps aux | grep -E 'falcon|crowd|cortex|sentinel|cylance|carbon' | grep -v grep

expected_artifacts.description: history -c / HISTFILE manipulation to clear shell history.
Access/truncation of /var/log/auth.log or syslog by a non-syslog process.
touch invoked with -t or -r to manipulate timestamps.
Shell binary copied to a kernel-thread style name.
Process table grep for EDR/AV agent names.

cleanup.code:
rm -f /tmp/cortexsim_auth_backup /tmp/cortexsim_fake_auth.log /tmp/cortexsim_timestomp_test
rm -f '/tmp/[kworker/0:1]' '/tmp/systemd-journald' /tmp/cortexsim_masq*
echo '[CortexSim] SIM-EDR-004 cleanup complete'

biocs.name: EDR-004 Bash History Clearing And HISTFILE Manipulation
biocs.description: Bash history clearing — HISTFILE manipulation and history -c execution detected. A shell unsets/redirects HISTFILE or truncates .bash_history.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("history -c", "HISTFILE=/dev/null", "unset HISTFILE", "> ~/.bash_history", "rm -f ~/.bash_history", "HISTSIZE=0")

biocs.name: EDR-004 System Log Access Or Truncation
biocs.description: System log file access and truncation attempt — log tampering to hide intrusion evidence. A non-syslog process reads, copies or truncates auth.log/syslog.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter action_file_path contains_any ("/var/log/auth.log", "/var/log/syslog", "/var/log/secure", "/var/log/messages", "/var/log/wtmp", "/var/log/btmp")
| filter actor_process_image_name not in ("rsyslogd", "syslog-ng", "systemd-journald", "logrotate", "auditd")

biocs.name: EDR-004 File Timestomp Via Touch
biocs.description: File timestamp modification via touch -t — timestomping to evade forensic analysis. touch is invoked with -t (explicit time) or -r (reference file).
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name = "touch"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any (" -t ", " -r ", " -d ", "--date", "--reference")

biocs.name: EDR-004 Binary Masquerade As Kernel Thread
biocs.description: Binary copied and renamed to match kernel thread name — process masquerading as kworker. A shell/binary is copied to a [kworker..]/systemd-* style name.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("cp", "mv", "install", "ln")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("[kworker", "[kthreadd]", "systemd-journald", "[migration", "[ksoftirqd")

biocs.name: EDR-004 Security Product Enumeration
biocs.description: Security product enumeration — process search for EDR/AV agent names indicates evasion preparation. The process table is grepped for common EDR/AV vendor strings.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("grep", "egrep", "pgrep", "ps")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("falcon", "crowdstrike", "crowd", "cortex", "traps", "sentinel", "cylance", "carbon", "defender", "clamav")

xql_queries.name: EDR-004 Analytics History Evasion Sequence
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("history -c", "HISTFILE", ".bash_history")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: EDR-004 Analytics Auth Log Access Non-Syslog Process
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE and action_file_path contains_any ("/var/log/auth.log", "/var/log/syslog")
| filter actor_process_image_name not in ("rsyslogd", "systemd-journald", "logrotate")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_process_image_name, action_file_path

xql_queries.name: EDR-004 Analytics Timestomp Reference File
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name = "touch"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any (" -r ", " -t ")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: EDR-004 Analytics Shell Binary Copied To System Name
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("cp", "mv")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("/bin/sh", "/bin/bash") and action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("kworker", "systemd-", "/tmp/")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: EDR-004 Analytics Security Tool Discovery Via Process Listing
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("grep", "egrep", "pgrep")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("falcon", "crowd", "cortex", "sentinel", "cylance", "carbon")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line

correlation_rules.name: Linux Anti-Forensics And Defense Evasion Chain
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(EDR-004 Bash History Clearing And HISTFILE Manipulation) OR BIOC(EDR-004 System Log Access Or Truncation) OR BIOC(EDR-004 File Timestomp Via Touch) OR BIOC(EDR-004 Binary Masquerade As Kernel Thread) OR BIOC(EDR-004 Security Product Enumeration)

analytics_modules: Cortex XDR Analytics — Anti-forensics activity on host
Cortex XDR Analytics — Security tooling reconnaissance

products.evidence_query: preset = xdr_data | filter actor_process_image_name = "touch" and action_process_image_command_line contains " -r " | fields _time, agent_hostname, action_process_image_command_line
Correlation CR-EDR-0004 fuses history-clear, log-tamper, timestomp, masquerade and tool-recon into one defense-evasion incident.
Playbook snapshots host forensics before the operator finishes covering tracks and isolates on correlation match.

use_cases.name: Defense Evasion Detection — Linux Anti-Forensics
use_cases.description: Detect log tampering, timestomping, masquerading and EDR reconnaissance on Linux even when the operator tries to erase evidence.

test_cases.objective: Each anti-forensics action fires its corresponding Cortex XDR BIOC.
preconditions: Cortex XDR Linux agent >= 8.4.0 installed and healthy.
BIOCs from this card imported and enabled.
steps: Observe per-action BIOC alerts in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: BIOC `EDR-004 File Timestomp Via Touch` fires within 60s.
BIOC `EDR-004 Security Product Enumeration` fires (gap-coverage BIOC).
All five XQL validation queries return >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Anti-forensics activity stitches into one defense-evasion incident.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-EDR-0004` groups the evasion actions on one host into a single incident.
Forensic snapshot is captured before evidence destruction completes.

preventive_controls: Forward all logs off-host in real time so local tampering does not destroy evidence.
Make /var/log append-only (chattr +a) and restrict writers to the logging daemons.
Enable Cortex XDR tamper protection so agent reconnaissance/kill attempts are blocked.
Use file-integrity monitoring on system binaries to catch masquerade copies.
detection_engineering: Alert on history -c / HISTFILE unset, on touch -t/-r, and on cp/mv to kernel-thread-style names.
Treat any non-logging-daemon write to auth.log/syslog as high-severity.
Flag process-table greps for EDR/AV vendor strings as evasion preparation.

references.title: Unit42 - xHunt Campaign: Attacks on Kuwait Shipping and Transportation
MITRE ATT&CK T1070.002 — Clear Linux or Mac System Logs
